{{Quote|You're all jealous of my abilities! Why shouldn't I be in control instead of the likes of you? You know I can do a better job than you! Admit it!|Dengless Rinn|<ref name="G11" />}}
'''Dengless Rinn''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] official of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], serving as the [[Prefect/Legends|prefect]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kallistas]]. An ambitious bureaucrat with a high opinion of his skills, Rinn coveted a higher rank. However, his superior, [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Linrec]], didn't share Rinn's estimate and thus did not offer Rinn any such promotion. Rinn hired [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to fight both the local agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], including Rinn's arch-nemesis [[Pallas Quintell]], and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] such as [[Jondrell Inx]]. He also resorted to these kinds of agents as part of a plot to topple and replace Linrec. The Empire discovered the scheme before it could succeed, and the civil servant became a fugitive. Authorities believed he was hiding in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

==Biography==
Dengless Rinn, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] and an official of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], was the Imperial [[Prefect/Legends|prefect]] of the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kallistas]]. He believed he was overqualified for his own job and desired a promotion. Rinn's superior, [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Linrec]], thought that this opinion was exaggerated and therefore did not offer the Prefect a promotion. Rinn saw Linrec as the individual responsible for his career stagnation and thus despised him. For [[Time/Legends|years]], Rinn's public nemesis was [[Pallas Quintell]], an agent of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Eventually, a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was posted on Quintell's head, and novice [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Sirenas Firil]] and [[Chandrex Grenn]] captured the Rebel.<ref name="G10" />

When Linrec arranged his daughter [[Cressis Linrec|Cressis's]] [[Marriage/Legends|wedding]] to [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Raiss]], Rinn, who considered himself a potential fiancé for her, thought that Governor Linrec had devised the wedding only to insult Rinn. To this end, the Prefect conspired against Linrec. Rinn contacted an organization of bounty hunters and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] of fortune, the [[Mantis Syndicate]], and made an order for a [[Company/Legends|company]] of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] that included demolition specialists. Mantis arranged a meeting between Rinn and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Tarquin Zian]], who could provide Rinn with several loads of weaponry and gear. In exchange for these services, Rinn offered Zian some [[Kallistan gem]]s that the Prefect had obtained illegally. Rinn also ordered his men to create [[fission bomb]]s from [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]] in the mines of Kallistas.<ref name="G10" />

Rinn intended to end the preparations and then declare himself dictator of Kallistas, despite the fact that such a move would prompt Linrec to send his [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to Kallistas to arrest Rinn. The Prefect intended to allow Linrec's soldiers to land, overwhelm them with his mercenaries, and threaten them with the bombs. Following his plan, the troops would then surrender, allowing Rinn to access their [[Transport/Legends|transport]]. The Mantis mercenaries would hijack the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and fly to [[Linrec's palace]], pretending to be Linrec's troops returning with a captured Rinn. Once they were in the palace, the mercenaries would take over the building and arrest Linrec on false charges. Rinn would then make a deal with any ambitious subordinate of Linrec to legitimize the governor's removal.<ref name="G10" />

Cressis, who disliked her fiancé, ran away with a local [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]], [[Jondrell Inx]]. Inx feigned interest, but he really wanted to sell her as a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] to the [[Zygerrian Slavers Guild]]. As Inx operated on Kallistas, Linrec notified Rinn. The governor ordered Rinn to find his daughter in three [[Time/Legends|days]], or Linrec's troops would be sent to Kallistas and take over the search. Rinn was afraid of Linrec discovering the preparations for his coup, so he decided to look for Cressis. To do so, Rinn hired three bounty hunters: [[Demos Traxen]], Sirenas Firil, and Chandrex Grenn. Believing that the hunters might betray him, Rinn asked Zian to watch them.<ref name="G10" />

Several bounty hunters foiled Rinn's conspiracy, which was discovered by the Empire. The Empire then issued an arrest warrant on Rinn for [[Treason/Legends|treason]], conspiracy, and illegal possession of weapons. Rinn quickly left Kallistas with little more than the clothes he was wearing at the moment—thereby adding flight to avoid prosecution to the charges against him. The law enforcement agencies believed that Rinn had used his smuggling contacts to hide in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], he had not been captured, although at that point an Imperial [[TracSheet]] on him, #66690933-USAW-854400, had been issued.<ref name="G11" />

==Personality and traits==
Dengless Rinn was noticeable for his long mustache and, to a lesser extent, his beard, severe [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and fierce expression. He also appeared to be older than he was. His Imperial TracSheet specified that Rinn was arrogant and had a sense of self-importance. He was not beyond threatening or [[Prison/Legends|imprisoning]] people at whim to make them obey his desires.<ref name="G11" /> Rinn was an experienced manager, financier, negotiator, and [[Politics/Legends|political]] leader. He was also a polyglot and had developed other abilities, including [[Computer/Legends|computer]] use, crime prevention, and [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]].<ref name="G11" /><ref name="G10" />

When he was a prefect, Rinn commonly carried a customized [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] that caused greater damage than expected, even though he had no experience with shooting. He also had a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] to communicate with the local security center in his possession.<ref name="G10" /> After leaving Kallistas, he lost the datapad and the custom gun. As a fugitive, he carried only the comlink, an off-the-shelf blaster pistol, and some [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|cash]].<ref name="G11" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dengless Rinn first appeared in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure ''[[Two for the Price of One]]'' by [[Rick D. Stuart]], published in ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', a [[1994]] supplement to [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The book included an image of Rinn drawn by [[Terry Pavlet]], which was used again in ''[[Galaxy Guide 11: Criminal Organizations]]'', published later in 1994. This second book provided a new set of updated role-playing stats for Rinn, representing his advanced experience and new knowledge obtained after leaving Kallistas.

''Galaxy Guide 11: Criminal Organizations'' describes Rinn as "tall and lanky." In the same box, Rinn's height is listed as 1.40 [[Meter/Legends|meters]], which is normally not considered tall for a Human male.
